anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: BG 2.21 is squarely inside the first thread: what survives change vs. what is current implementation. The engineering layer's spec-vs-implementation framing IS the chapter's architectural-invariants thread, applied to the imperishable doctrine specifically. The four-property quartet maps directly to the chapter's hard-edge claim: 'the interface survives even when the implementation churns; the spec survives even when the code rewrites.'

progression_issue.explanation: Position 21 is the rhetorical climax of the imperishable-properties block (2.19-2.21). The engineering layer correctly stays at first-principles register and does not preempt the karma-yoga block (2.31+) by introducing shipping-discipline language.

verdict_reason: BG 2.21 is in the chapter's sāṅkhya thread, applied to its rhetorical climax. The engineering layer respects the chapter's hard edges: the atman-survives-change claim is preserved as 'the contract is the work; the implementation is its clothing,' which is the chapter thesis statement nearly verbatim. The verse is correctly tagged HIGH confidence (the chapter sanctioned this exact engineering analog as load-bearing). No tactical-panic register, no productivity-book softening. PASS.
